message:
perf(fcp2): reduce allocations in CBOR hashing, canonicalization, FCPS framing, and RaptorQ codec; add bench profile

Cohesive performance pass across the FCP2 wire-format crates, targeting
heap allocations in hot encoding/hashing/serialization paths.

Cargo.toml:
- Add [profile.bench] with opt-level=3, lto=true, codegen-units=1.
  The release profile uses opt-level="z" (size-optimized), which is
  2-5x slower for compute-heavy paths. Benchmarks now use speed-optimized
  codegen for accurate throughput measurement.

fcp-cbor/lib.rs:
- SchemaId::hash() previously called self.as_bytes() which allocates a
  temporary Vec<u8> to build the full "{namespace}:{name}@{version}"
  string. Now feeds each component directly into the BLAKE3 hasher
  (namespace, ":", name, "@", version.to_string()). The hash result is
  identical (same byte sequence) but avoids the intermediate allocation.

fcp-crypto/canonicalize.rs:
- In canonicalize_map(), hoist key_buf allocation outside the loop.
  Instead of allocating a new Vec::new() per map entry for CBOR key
  serialization, reuse a single Vec::with_capacity(64) buffer, calling
  .clear() each iteration. The .clone() for storing key bytes is still
  needed, but the serialization buffer itself is now reused.

fcp-protocol/fcps.rs:
- Add #[inline] to FcpsFrameHeader::encode(), SymbolRecord::wire_size(),
  SymbolRecord::encode(), and SymbolRecord::decode()
- Add new method SymbolRecord::encode_to(&self, buf: &mut Vec<u8>) that
  encodes directly into an existing buffer (zero per-symbol allocation)
- Change FcpsFrame::encode() to call symbol.encode_to(&mut buf) instead
  of symbol.encode() + extend_from_slice, eliminating a temporary Vec
  allocation per symbol

fcp-raptorq/decode.rs:
- Replace .clone() assignments with .clone_from() in two places to reuse
  existing allocations instead of alloc+dealloc per symbol
- Replace manual for-i-in-0..k_prime loop with idiomatic
  iter().enumerate().take()
- Replace .ok_or(DecodeError{...}) with .ok_or_else(|| ...) to avoid
  eagerly constructing the error struct on the happy path
- Merge duplicated match arms in map_inner_error()

fcp-raptorq/encode.rs:
- Add two new consuming methods: into_encode_all() and
  into_encode_source() that move source data directly instead of
  cloning, for callers that don't need the encoder after encoding

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -329,3 +329,12 @@ lto = true
 codegen-units = 1
 panic = "abort"
 strip = true
+
+# Benchmark profile: inherit release settings but optimize for speed, not size.
+# `cargo bench` uses this automatically. This prevents benchmarks from measuring
+# size-optimized codegen (`opt-level = "z"`) which is 2-5x slower than speed-
+# optimized codegen for compute-heavy paths (RaptorQ, crypto, CBOR).
+[profile.bench]
+opt-level = 3
+lto = true
+codegen-units = 1
